endpoint_coverage.notes: All major API endpoints present: auth (4), projects (8), jobs (6), upload (3), content (4), qa (5), prompts (5), templates (3), workflow (5). Total: 43 endpoints across 9 route files.

input_validation.notes: CRITICAL: Most endpoints use placeholder implementations with TODO comments. Pydantic models are well-defined for requests/responses (ContentGenerateRequest, QACompareRequest, PromptCreate, etc.), but actual validation logic not implemented. Form data validation in upload.py is manual rather than using Pydantic models. Template type and variant validation is hardcoded in multiple places - should be centralized.

authentication.notes: CRITICAL: Auth middleware exists (app.middleware.auth.get_current_user) and is applied to all protected routes. However, app/api/dependencies.py contains placeholder NotImplementedError for both get_db() and get_current_user(). Actual implementation is in app.middleware.auth which imports from app.services.auth_service. Inconsistent dependency - some routes import from middleware.auth, others from api.dependencies (which doesn't work). Admin role checks are commented as TODO in multiple places (prompts.py, workflow.py).

error_handling.notes: Consistent error format used across all endpoints with structured detail dicts containing error_code, message, and details. Appropriate status codes: 400 for validation, 401/403 for auth, 404 for not found, 422 for semantic errors, 500 for server errors. Try-catch blocks properly re-raise HTTPException. No stack traces leaked in responses. However, trace_id NOT included in error responses as claimed in spec.

documentation.notes: Excellent OpenAPI documentation. All endpoints have summary, description, and detailed docstrings. Pydantic models auto-generate schema. Response models properly defined. Examples could be added to Pydantic Field() definitions for better OpenAPI spec.

rate_limiting.notes: CRITICAL: Rate limiting NOT implemented. upload.py line 115 has TODO comment for rate limit checks. No middleware or dependencies for rate limiting visible. No 429 responses implemented. No X-RateLimit headers present.

security.notes: Input sanitization present: UUID validation prevents injection, file type validation via content_type, file size limits enforced. Form inputs use Pydantic Field validation. No SQL injection risk (using SQLAlchemy ORM with async sessions). XSS risk minimal as API returns JSON. Detail dicts in errors are controlled, not echoing raw user input. upload.py reads file content into memory which could be DoS vector for large files - should stream to storage instead.

performance.notes: MAJOR ISSUES: (1) Pagination implemented in projects.py and workflow.py but NOT in prompts.py list_prompts (returns unbounded results). (2) No N+1 query analysis possible as database layer is TODO. (3) upload.py line 83 reads entire file into memory - should stream. (4) No query timeouts configured at route level. (5) templates.py mentions caching (lines 97, 188) but not implemented. (6) No Content-Length limits on request bodies except file uploads.

runtime_validation.details: Import test will fail due to missing implementations. Dependencies in app.api.dependencies raise NotImplementedError. app.config imports (get_settings, setup_logging, check_database_connection) not verified to exist. app.services imports (auth_service, user_service, project_service, job_manager) not verified. app.repositories imports not verified. All route handlers are async which is correct. No blocking time.sleep() or requests calls detected. Type hints present on most functions but some missing return type annotations.

issues.description: get_db() raises NotImplementedError - all database operations will fail
issues.recommendation: Implement proper async session factory using app.config.database.async_session_maker as shown in comment

issues.description: get_current_user() raises NotImplementedError but routes import from here
issues.recommendation: Remove this placeholder file or have it re-export from app.middleware.auth. Update all routes to import from app.middleware.auth consistently

issues.description: No rate limiting implemented despite TODO comments
issues.recommendation: Implement slowapi or fastapi-limiter middleware. Apply limits: 100/min for read endpoints, 20/min for write endpoints, 10/min for upload endpoints

issues.description: Entire file read into memory (await file.read()) - DoS risk for large files
issues.recommendation: Stream file directly to GCS using resumable uploads. Validate size from Content-Length header before reading

issues.description: list_prompts has no pagination - will return unbounded results
issues.recommendation: Add page and limit query parameters like other list endpoints

issues.description: Template type validation hardcoded in 5+ files (aggregators, opr, mpp, adop, adre, commercial)
issues.recommendation: Create Enum in app.models.enums.TemplateType and import everywhere. Single source of truth

issues.description: Form data not validated with Pydantic - manual validation required
issues.recommendation: Create UploadPDFRequest Pydantic model with File and Form fields. FastAPI supports this via Form(...) and File(...)

issues.description: Error responses don't include trace_id for debugging
issues.recommendation: Add middleware to inject trace_id (UUID) into request context, include in all error responses

issues.description: Content-Type validation can be spoofed - validates header not actual content
issues.recommendation: Use python-magic or filetype library to detect actual file type from bytes, not just Content-Type header

issues.description: Pydantic Field() definitions lack examples for OpenAPI
issues.recommendation: Add examples to Field definitions: Field(..., example='opr')

issues.description: ContentResponse includes timestamp as string, not datetime
issues.recommendation: Use datetime type and let FastAPI serialize to ISO format automatically

issues.description: Comment mentions caching but not implemented
issues.recommendation: Implement caching with @cache decorator or Redis for template data

issues.description: Workflow status transition validation commented as TODO
issues.recommendation: Implement state machine validation to prevent invalid transitions

issues.description: Inconsistent prefix patterns - /api for auth, /api/v1 for others
issues.recommendation: Consider moving auth to /api/v1/auth for consistency or document the rationale

issues.description: search_projects uses @router.get('/search') which won't work - conflicts with /{project_id}
issues.recommendation: Move to /api/v1/search/projects or use query param on list endpoint

recommendations.details: Implement database session management, authentication dependencies, and rate limiting before proceeding. Current code cannot run.
Create enums for TemplateType, ContentVariant, WorkflowStatus, CheckpointType. Create shared validation utilities. Reduces duplication across 9 route files.
Change upload.py to stream files directly to GCS without loading into memory. Use google-cloud-storage resumable uploads.
Create tests for each endpoint covering: happy path, validation errors, auth errors, edge cases. Use pytest-asyncio and httpx.AsyncClient.
Document whether breaking changes require new version. Consider deprecation headers (Sunset, Deprecation) for future changes.
Log request_id, method, path, status_code, duration, user_id. Essential for debugging and monitoring.
Enhances auto-generated docs and makes API more discoverable. Add to all request/response models.
Projects API has many filters. GraphQL could provide more flexible querying. Strawberry + FastAPI integration is straightforward.

positive_findings: Excellent API organization with clear separation by domain (auth, projects, jobs, upload, content, qa, prompts, templates, workflow)
Consistent error response structure across all endpoints
Well-documented endpoints with docstrings and OpenAPI metadata
Proper async/await patterns throughout - no blocking calls detected
Good use of Pydantic for request/response validation
UUID validation prevents SQL injection on ID parameters
Appropriate HTTP status codes used consistently
File upload validation includes type and size checks
Try-catch blocks properly handle exceptions without leaking internals
Jobs API has good separation of concerns with dedicated service layer
test_coverage_requirements: Unit tests for each Pydantic model validation
Integration tests for each endpoint (happy path + error cases)
Auth middleware tests (valid token, expired token, invalid token, no token)
Rate limiting tests (under limit, at limit, over limit)
File upload tests (valid file, invalid type, oversized file, corrupted file)
Pagination tests (first page, middle page, last page, out of bounds)
Search tests (relevance ranking, special characters, SQL injection attempts)
Error handling tests (network timeout, database connection lost, external API down)

strengths: UUID validation on all ID parameters prevents SQL injection
File type and size validation on uploads
Password hashing assumed in auth service (not visible in routes)
Secure flag enabled in production for cookies (auth.py:128)
No raw SQL queries visible - using ORM
vulnerabilities: No rate limiting - vulnerable to brute force and DoS
File content-type validation relies on client header, not actual file inspection
No CSRF protection visible (acceptable for JWT auth but should be documented)
No input length limits on text fields (e.g., custom field values)
File upload loads entire file into memory - memory exhaustion DoS vector
No mention of API key rotation strategy
CORS allows all methods (*) - should be restrictive in production

concerns: No pagination on prompts.py list endpoint - unbounded results
File upload reads entire file into memory - should stream
No caching layer despite comments mentioning it
projects.py search endpoint may cause full table scans
Workflow board endpoint (workflow.py:73) loads all items - no pagination
recommendations: Add Redis caching for templates, prompts, and static reference data
Implement database connection pooling with max size limits
Add query timeouts at database level
Use streaming for file uploads to cloud storage
Implement pagination on all list endpoints
Add database indexes on frequently queried fields (workflow_status, created_at, user_id)
Consider read replicas for heavy read endpoints

next_steps: 1. Implement database session management and authentication (BLOCKER)
6. Write integration tests for critical paths (QUALITY)
10. Perform load testing on upload and list endpoints (VALIDATION)
